name: Amble
shortBio: Irish folk trio (Robbie Cunningham, Ross McNerney, Oisín McCaffrey) with soaring three-part harmonies and cinematic acoustic songs that became a fast-rising live draw.

name: Belle Blue
shortBio: A rising songwriter bringing intimate, melodic folk to the Bike Stage.

name: Brandi Carlile
shortBio: Nine-time Grammy winner and Newport favorite, a powerhouse Americana voice and festival curator; her 'Returning to Myself' set spotlights new work.

name: Brandon Flowers
shortBio: Frontman of The Killers, bringing widescreen, heartland-tinged rock anthems and a commanding stage presence to his solo material.

name: Brittany Davis
shortBio: Seattle multi-instrumentalist and songwriter whose genre-fluid soul, funk and spoken word is fierce and cinematic; performing with Black Thunder.

name: Brother Wallace
shortBio: Georgia-raised soul artist who began playing piano in church, teaming with The Heavy's Dan Taylor for heartfelt, gospel-touched soul.

name: Case Oats
shortBio: A Chicago country and honky-tonk band fronted by songwriter Casey Walker, with warm, classic-leaning twang.

name: Cat Power
shortBio: Chan Marshall's revered indie project, spanning smoky soul, spare folk and reinventions of classic songbooks over three decades.

name: Clover County
shortBio: A roots and country band delivering easy, harmony-rich Americana.

name: CMAT
shortBio: Irish pop sensation Ciara Mary-Alice Thompson, delivering witty, theatrical country-pop with huge hooks and bigger personality.

name: Community Chapstick
shortBio: A collaborative Foundation Stage set from the festival's community and education programming.

name: Courtney Barnett
shortBio: Australian singer-songwriter celebrated for deadpan, stream-of-consciousness lyrics and loose garage-rock guitar. A festival favorite since her breakout 'Sometimes I Sit and Think...'

name: Courtney Marie Andrews
shortBio: Arizona-born songwriter with a crystalline voice and country-folk songs about longing and the American West. A Grammy-nominated storyteller.

name: Dawes
shortBio: Los Angeles band led by brothers Taylor and Griffin Goldsmith, torchbearers of Laurel Canyon folk-rock with sharp songwriting; performing here as a duo.

name: Deer Tick
shortBio: Providence, RI's beloved ragged rock-and-roots band led by John McCauley; a Newport staple whose 'and Friends' sets are famous for guest-packed chaos.

name: Dove Ellis
shortBio: An emerging songwriter bringing soulful folk to the Quad Stage.

name: Evan Honer
shortBio: Arizona-raised songwriter blending modern country and folk, who built a big audience online before hitting festival stages.

name: For Pete's Sake
shortBio: A morning Foundation Stage gathering in the spirit of Pete Seeger — communal singing and songs led by a rotating host.

name: Fruit Bats
shortBio: Eric D. Johnson's long-running indie-folk project, trading in bright melodies, warm keyboards and road-worn Americana. Also a member of Bonny Light Horseman.

name: Gillian Welch & David Rawlings
shortBio: The revered Americana duo of Gillian Welch and David Rawlings, masters of spare Appalachian harmony, here reinterpreting the Grateful Dead's acoustic songbook.

name: Gwenifer Raymond
shortBio: Welsh guitarist and banjoist working in the American Primitive tradition, spinning intense, virtuosic solo instrumentals.

name: Haley Heynderickx & Max García Conover
shortBio: Portland songwriter Haley Heynderickx and Maine folk artist Max García Conover, whose collaborative record trades intimate, intertwined guitar and voice.

name: Hayley Williams
shortBio: Frontwoman of Paramore and acclaimed solo artist, bringing pop-punk energy and confessional pop songwriting. Her Newport 'and Friends' set promises collaborations.

name: Honest Charlie
shortBio: A roots and folk artist bringing warm, unhurried songs to open the Fort Stage.

name: Hot Tuna
shortBio: The long-running blues and roots duo/band of Jorma Kaukonen and Jack Casady, founding members of Jefferson Airplane, playing electric and acoustic Americana since 1969.

name: Hudson Freeman
shortBio: A young singer-songwriter and Newport mentee, part of the festival's education programming, with earnest acoustic songs.

name: Infinity Song
shortBio: New York soft-rock band of siblings Abraham, Angel, Israel and Momo Boyd, signed to Roc Nation, known for shimmering harmonies and a viral hit in 'Hater's Anthem.'

name: Jackie Evans
shortBio: An emerging songwriter featured in the festival's Foundation Stage programming.

name: John R. Miller
shortBio: West Virginia songwriter and guitarist writing weathered, detail-rich country and folk in a classic honky-tonk tradition.

name: Jonathan Bernstein
shortBio: A songwriter featured on the Foundation Stage.

name: Jordan Klepper
shortBio: Comedian and 'The Daily Show' correspondent, bringing a talk-and-comedy set to the Foundation Stage.

name: Kathleen Edwards
shortBio: Canadian alt-country songwriter known for sharp, emotionally direct songs and a return to music after stepping away to run a coffee shop.

name: Kirby
shortBio: A soul and R&B singer-songwriter (and acclaimed hitmaker) with a rich, classic voice.

name: Leif Vollebekk
shortBio: Montreal songwriter crafting hushed, soulful piano-and-guitar folk with a jazz-inflected sense of space and groove.

name: Lily Fitts
shortBio: A young songwriter and emerging talent playing a short Bike Stage set.

name: Lizzy McAlpine
shortBio: Philadelphia singer-songwriter whose intimate, jazz-tinged folk-pop and the hit 'ceilings' made her a streaming and touring phenomenon.

name: Lucy Dacus
shortBio: Virginia-raised indie-rock songwriter and boygenius member known for literate, slow-burning songs and a warm alto. Her 2025 album 'Forever Is a Feeling' widened her sound toward lush chamber-pop.

name: Madi Diaz
shortBio: Nashville songwriter whose unflinching heartbreak songs and clear voice earned acclaim and high-profile touring with Harry Styles.

name: Mark Cutler
shortBio: A pillar of the Rhode Island music scene, singer-songwriter Mark Cutler brings decades of local roots-rock craft.

name: Matt Quinn
shortBio: A songwriter delivering easygoing, melodic folk-rock on the Harbor Stage.

name: Medium Build
shortBio: Alaska-raised, Atlanta-based Nick Carpenter's indie-rock project, mixing raw confessional lyrics with big, textured production.

name: MET Lab Students
shortBio: Young musicians from the Newport Festivals Foundation's education programming, performing with mentor Hudson Freeman.

name: Michael Shannon & Jason Narducy and Friends Play R.E.M.
shortBio: Actor Michael Shannon and musician Jason Narducy lead a rotating band performing R.E.M. albums in full — a joyful, faithful tribute.

name: Morgan Nagler
shortBio: Songwriter and frontperson of Whispertown, writing candid, melodic indie-folk.

name: Ms. Lauryn Hill
shortBio: Grammy-winning icon whose 'The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill' reshaped hip-hop and soul. A rare festival appearance from one of music's defining voices.

name: Music Lab Students
shortBio: Students from the festival's music-education Lab take the Foundation Stage with mentor Taylor Goldsmith of Dawes.

name: Nabeel
shortBio: An emerging singer-songwriter closing out the Bike Stage lineup.

name: Nathaniel Rateliff
shortBio: Denver soul-rock singer, with and without The Night Sweats, whose gravelly voice powers barn-burners like 'S.O.B.' and tender solo balladry.

name: Open Mic
shortBio: The Bike Stage's daily open-mic slot, giving festivalgoers and up-and-comers a chance to play a few songs to start the day.

name: Peter Rowan / Sam Grisman / Sierra Hull / Larry Campbell & Teresa Williams
shortBio: A bluegrass and roots summit uniting Peter Rowan, Sam Grisman, mandolin virtuoso Sierra Hull, and the duo Larry Campbell & Teresa Williams.

name: Princess June
shortBio: An up-and-coming act opening the Fort Stage with bright, melodic songs.

name: Punch Brothers
shortBio: Genre-bending acoustic quintet led by mandolinist Chris Thile, pushing bluegrass into classical, jazz and pop with dazzling interplay.

name: Ryan Davis & The Roadhouse Band
shortBio: Louisville songwriter Ryan Davis (State Champion) leading sprawling, wry, guitar-driven country-rock epics.

name: Sally Rose
shortBio: A songwriter bringing heartfelt Americana to the Bike Stage.

name: Sea to Shining Sea
shortBio: A Newport special set gathering artists for a collaborative journey through American song — the kind of one-off the festival is known for.

name: Searows
shortBio: Alec Duckart's project, hushed and devastating indie-folk built on fingerpicked guitar and whispered, diaristic lyrics.

name: Snacktime
shortBio: A Philadelphia brass-and-groove collective known for joyful, danceable street-band energy.

name: Stephen Covell
shortBio: A veteran and songwriter performing through Operation Encore, the program supporting military-veteran musicians.

name: Strongboi
shortBio: An indie-pop duo with playful, hook-forward songs and a quirky charm.

name: Taylor Hollingsworth
shortBio: Alabama guitarist and songwriter (a Conor Oberst collaborator) with scrappy, garage-leaning country-rock.

name: Father John Misty
shortBio: The satirical, orchestral-folk project of Josh Tillman (former Fleet Foxes drummer), whose grand, wry songwriting runs from Fear Fun and I Love You, Honeybear through 2024's Mahashmashana.

name: The Barr Brothers
shortBio: Montreal band led by Brad and Andrew Barr, blending fingerstyle folk, blues and harp-driven chamber textures into atmospheric rock.

name: The Fearless Flyers
shortBio: Instrumental funk supergroup from the Vulf family — Cory Wong, Joe Dart, Mark Lettieri and Nate Smith — locked into irresistible grooves.

name: The Lumineers
shortBio: Denver folk-rock band behind stadium-sized singalongs like 'Ho Hey' and 'Ophelia,' blending stomping acoustic anthems with intimate storytelling.

name: The Olllam
shortBio: Transatlantic collective co-founded by uilleann piper John McSherry and multi-instrumentalist Tyler Duncan, weaving trance-like Celtic melodies with jazz and funk.

name: This Is Lorelei
shortBio: The solo project of Nate Amos (Water From Your Eyes), swerving from lo-fi bedroom pop to warped country on the acclaimed 'Box for Buddy, Box for Star.'

name: Tim Bernardes
shortBio: Brazilian songwriter and O Terno frontman crafting gorgeous, orchestral MPB folk with a delicate falsetto; a Fleet Foxes and Devendra Banhart collaborator.

name: Tiny Habits
shortBio: A Boston-formed trio built on tight three-part harmony and gentle acoustic arrangements, who grew a following from viral cover videos into original indie-folk.

name: Tom Morello
shortBio: Rage Against the Machine and Audioslave guitarist and activist, reinventing protest music through electrified riffs and, increasingly, acoustic folk.

name: Trousdale
shortBio: Los Angeles trio prized for airtight harmonies and folk-pop songwriting, built from years of singing together.

name: Victoria Canal
shortBio: Spanish-American singer-songwriter and Ivor Novello winner writing soulful, piano-driven pop with candid lyricism about identity and disability.

name: Vulfpeck
shortBio: Ann Arbor funk band famous for pocket grooves, minimalist production and joyful live shows that turned a studio project into arena-fillers.

name: Wednesday
shortBio: Asheville, NC band fusing shoegaze, alt-country and 90s indie into loud, lap-steel-soaked rock. Fronted by songwriter Karly Hartzman.

name: Yasmin Williams & William Tyler
shortBio: A meeting of two of instrumental guitar's most inventive voices — Yasmin Williams' percussive, lap-tapped playing and William Tyler's cinematic fingerstyle.
